demographics.gender_identity: transgender / gender-diverse — experienced gender differs from sex assigned at birth
demographics.living_situation: shares a home with a roommate, who is present and helping the patient during the call

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I can't look at it, I can't feel it, I just need this body to stop existing the way it does right now. My chest is killing me and I can't take a proper breath.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: I bound my chest really tight for a long time to flatten it and now my ribs are bruised and it hurts so much I can't breathe in properly, and I can't calm down.

symptom_narrative.onset: over the last day or two it built up, and it got really bad in the last few hours after I had the binding on for a long time
symptom_narrative.duration: the chest pain and tight breathing have been going for a few hours and aren't easing
symptom_narrative.character: a deep aching, bruised soreness across my ribs and breastbone, and my breathing feels squeezed and shallow — it hurts to take a full breath
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: it's really bad, I can't get comfortable and I can't stop crying
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: across the sides of my ribs and the middle of my chest
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant, worse when I try to breathe in deeply or move
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing much yet — taking the binding off helps the squeezing a bit but the soreness stays
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: breathing in deeply, moving, and having anything tight on my chest
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: my heart feels like it's racing, I feel shaky and panicky, and I feel strangely disconnected — like I'm watching my own body from outside it
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I've bound before but never hurt myself like this
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I can't really do anything right now, I can't settle, I haven't been able to look after myself today

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: ongoing low mood and distress tied to how my body looks and being misgendered

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: I've been withdrawing from people because being misgendered is so distressing, and I get very anxious about my voice in public. I bind my chest to try to flatten it because I can't stand how it looks, and today I kept it on far too tight for far too long.

objective_data_offered.value: deep red pressure marks, bruising and a few surface grazes across the sides of the ribs and the breastbone from tight binding materials (tape / tight garment)
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: seen on video only; cannot assess for rib fracture or deeper injury without an in-person exam

objective_data_offered.value: breathing looks fast and shallow, and the patient winces and guards when trying to breathe in deeply
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: visual impression only; true respiratory rate and air entry cannot be measured over video

objective_data_offered.value: very distressed and crying continuously, avoiding eye contact, sitting hunched over and holding the chest
the bedroom mirrors are covered with dark blankets or broken; used binding tape and a tight garment are on the floor
